summary: Frontend-only Playwright test of the Orlog Vite/R3F app. Solo (vs Æsir → Skald) flow tested end-to-end: home → solo → game scene (3D + HUDs) → roll dice → lock dice → forfeit → end screen → back to home. All critical data-testid elements visible & clickable; no JS pageerrors; zero React/Three runtime errors. Only minor non-blocking issues: 3 ERR_BLOCKED_BY_RESPONSE.NotSameOrigin resource errors (likely placeholder Supabase / external asset CORS) and THREE.js deprecation warnings (Clock, PCFSoftShadowMap).

ui_bugs.issue: User-side dice still rendered as '?' faces ~3.5s after clicking roll-dice-button; Lock Dice button correctly enabled (logic works) but face reveal visually delayed. Could be 3D cup-descent animation length or selfHasRolled flag transition. Worth verifying timing.

issues: Initial 3D scene takes ~3-5s to fully composite (procedural texture generation) — first screenshot showed black canvas; subsequent screenshot shows full table with bowls, torches, runic ring (looks great).
post_round.png confirms beautiful Viking aesthetic: wooden table, glowing iron-bound HUDs, flickering torches in corners, atmospheric vignette/bloom.

console_errors_observed: Failed to load resource: net::ERR_BLOCKED_BY_RESPONSE.NotSameOrigin (x9) — likely placeholder Supabase REST or CORS-protected external asset. Non-blocking.

tests_executed.game_scene_3d: PASS (game-scene-3d visible, HUDs, scene visuals confirmed in screenshot)
tests_executed.player_huds: PASS (player-hud-host + player-hud-guest both visible with HP bars + favor)
tests_executed.roll_dice: PASS — button clickable, no JS errors; visual face-reveal timing slightly slow
tests_executed.lock_dice: PASS — stand-button enabled after turnRolled, advances game
tests_executed.saga_log: PASS — resolution-log visible with at least one entry ('The game begins. May the Norns favor you.')
tests_executed.forfeit_flow: PASS — leave-game-button → end screen 'FORFEIT' → home-button returns to home (create-session-button visible again)
tests_executed.phase_banner: INCONCLUSIVE — checked too early; banner only triggers on phase transitions and is brief, not seen but no error
tests_executed.audio_no_js_errors: PASS — 0 pageerrors during audio cue triggers
tests_executed.no_red_console_errors_blocking: PASS — only non-blocking CORS resource errors and Three.js deprecation warnings

action_items: Investigate the 3 ERR_BLOCKED_BY_RESPONSE.NotSameOrigin resource errors. If they originate from the placeholder Supabase URL fetches in solo mode, gate those network calls behind the !aiMode condition so solo play makes zero external requests.
OPTIONAL: Bump @react-three/fiber/drei or replace deprecated THREE.Clock with THREE.Timer; switch shadow map type from PCFSoftShadowMap to PCFShadowMap to silence warnings.

OPTIONAL: Verify dice face-reveal timing after Shake & Roll — consider shortening cup-descent animation or revealing 2D dice faces sooner so users see immediate feedback (currently faces remained '?' ~3.5s after click in headless test).
critical_code_review_comments: GameScreen.tsx (342 lines): consider splitting the dice-tray + saga-log + reconnect-banner subviews into sibling components — file is approaching the 700-line threshold and is densely packed.
Solo mode still appears to trigger external Supabase requests (per CORS errors). Should short-circuit to local channel when aiMode is set, before any network init.

Phase banner trigger only fires for 'roll'/'favor'/'resolve' transitions and not on the very first round-1 roll entry (because lastPhase.current is initialized to snap.phase). If desired, force the banner once on mount for cinematic effect.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Solo flow stable. The 3D scene needs ~3-5s to fully render. Use leave-game-button to forfeit (end screen shows 'FORFEIT' for self-forfeit, with home-button data-testid returning to home). Dice face reveal after Shake&Roll appears delayed visually (~3.5s) but logic is correct (turnRolled flips → Lock Dice enables). All key data-testid attributes are present & functional.
